title: Deed.
content:
A deed knocks first at thought,
    And then it knocks at will.
    That is the manufacturing spot,
    And will at home and well.

    It then goes out an act,
    Or is entombed so still
    That only to the ear of God
    Its doom is audible.
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Deed."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
